notes: Central to Nortel's multimedia vision. ATM was eventually displaced by Ethernet/MPLS/IP for enterprise networking. Nortel's ATM focus was a strategic risk.
Nortel's flagship ATM switching product line. Claimed #1 in multimedia networking. Passport enabled multimedia voice/data/video; documented customer savings of $435,000/year.
X.25 and TDM packet switches. Popular in traditional WAN deployments.
Nortel's flagship PBX product line for enterprise voice. Became Avaya product after 2009 asset sale.
Standard WAN protocol at time of study. Customer testimonial involved 10-county frame relay network.
Referenced as a WAN service option. VPN became a mainstream enterprise technology.
ITU standard for videoconferencing over ISDN. Nortel offered H.320 network applications. Superseded by IP-based video (H.323, SIP, WebRTC).
Integration of telephony with computing. Nortel highlighted CTI as a differentiator. CTI became ubiquitous in call centers.
Fundamental enterprise networking concept discussed throughout study.
Referenced as providing survivability for six-sigma reliability in Nortel ATM networks.
